$description: Dark mode semantic color overrides. Source: Patina globals.css .dark block. Only tokens that differ from light mode are listed.

primary.$description: Inverted — light gray as primary in dark mode

border.$description: Semi-transparent white border in dark mode

input.$description: Slightly more visible input borders in dark mode

sidebar-primary.$description: Same as light — pastel-red for sidebar active

section-hook-bg.$description: Hook section in dark mode. Tinted-dark using midnight-purple hue to differentiate from page bg (neutral-950).

section-context-bg.$description: Context section in dark mode. One step lighter than page bg.

section-evidence-bg.$description: Evidence section in dark mode. Same as page bg — clean separation comes from content, not color.

section-turn-bg-from.$description: Turn gradient start in dark mode. Slightly more visible than light mode.

section-turn-bg-to.$description: Turn gradient end in dark mode.

section-turn-border.$description: Turn border — same in both modes.

section-close-bg.$description: Close section in dark mode. Even darker midnight-purple tint — distinct from both hook and page bg.

wave-color-low.$description: Same as light mode. Waves always render on dark hook backgrounds.

wave-color-high.$description: Same as light mode. Waves always render on dark hook backgrounds.

wave-opacity.$description: Same as light mode. Waves always render on dark hook backgrounds.
